title: Conflicts are jolting energy systems. Will they cause ‘structural breaks’?
gist: Panelists at the Atlantic Council Global Energy Forum argue the Ukraine and Iran wars are reshaping energy security toward survivability over efficiency, with the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz hitting jet/diesel demand while gasoline holds. Central/Eastern Europe is diversifying via reversed pipelines, renewables, storage and SMRs, while the open question is how long the Hormuz closure can persist before causing permanent 'structural breaks'.

insights.claim: AECOM's Karl Jensen argues the central lesson of the Ukraine war is that survivability—not efficiency—must be the organizing principle for energy systems, citing Russia's systematic targeting of Ukrainian power plants and grids.
insights.speaker: srothbardt
insights.so_what: Reframes energy capex toward redundancy/hardening over least-cost—favors distributed generation, storage, and grid-resilience suppliers.
insights.quote: Green will always be part of the future of energy, but you can’t measure our future energy system on how it performs on the best day

insights.claim: Romania has deployed more than seven gigawatts of renewables, expects two gigawatts of storage online by end-2026, and is advancing a 462-MW NuScale SMR at the former Doicești coal plant that took a final investment decision in February 2026.
insights.speaker: srothbardt
insights.so_what: A real FID makes the Doicești NuScale project a leading near-term SMR datapoint—watch for execution as an SMR-sector proof point.

insights.claim: Bulgaria's Glavbolgarstroy is a strategic co-investor in Blue Bird Energy, a JV aiming to deploy up to six GE Vernova Hitachi BWRX-300 reactors in Bulgaria.
insights.speaker: srothbardt
insights.so_what: Concrete BWRX-300 deployment pipeline—a demand signal for GE Vernova's SMR order book in CEE.

insights.claim: Romania will begin exporting gas to the region when the offshore Neptune Deep field comes online in 2027, and Romania's regulator argues gas and renewables should complement rather than compete.
insights.speaker: srothbardt
insights.so_what: Neptune Deep flips Romania to a regional gas exporter in 2027—a supply-side shift for CEE gas balances.
insights.quote: natural gas is flowing west, not from the east.

insights.claim: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz is disaggregating oil demand by product: Kpler's Reid I'Anson says gasoline is holding up while jet and diesel (middle distillates) demand is weak.
insights.speaker: srothbardt
insights.so_what: Trade the crack spread split—long gasoline vs. weak distillate demand—rather than a uniform crude view during the crisis.
insights.quote: we are seeing these disaggregations on the demand side

insights.claim: ConocoPhillips chief economist Helen Currie argues it is too early to call long-term oil/gas demand destruction, characterizing current moves as 'adjustments,' and stresses capital allocation as the first-order long-term concern.
insights.speaker: srothbardt
insights.so_what: A major producer resisting the demand-destruction narrative—relevant to upstream capex and long-dated oil demand bets.
insights.quote: Demand is making adjustments—I don’t know if I’d call it destruction.

insights.claim: Kpler's I'Anson frames the key macro risk as how long the Hormuz closure can persist before causing permanent 'structural breaks' in the energy system.
insights.speaker: srothbardt
insights.so_what: Duration is the variable that converts a price shock into permanent demand/supply reconfiguration—the key thing to monitor.
insights.quote: how much longer can [the Strait of Hormuz closure] go on for before you see structural breaks?

insights.claim: Excelerate Energy's Alisa Newman Hood says US LNG exports now constitute about 30 percent of all natural gas supply for India, Bangladesh, and Pakistan—triple their prior share—and questions whether importers have enough regasification capacity for the coming LNG wave.
insights.speaker: srothbardt
insights.so_what: Regas capacity in South Asia is the bottleneck for absorbing US LNG—an FSRU/regas infrastructure investment thesis (Excelerate's own business).
insights.quote: Do importers have the regasification capacity needed to take advantage of this LNG wave we’re expecting?

insights.claim: İhlas Holding's Ahmet Oren says grid load equivalent to the size of the entire Japanese grid is being added every year, and positions renewables plus hydropower as the answer to the data-center demand challenge.
insights.speaker: srothbardt
insights.so_what: A vivid sizing of AI/data-center power demand growth—reinforces the structural power-demand and generation-capacity theme.
insights.quote: the size of the Japanese grid is being loaded onto the grid every year
